agents.empty: No agents yet? Create your first AI assistant!

agents.delegate.placeholder: Describe the task you want to delegate...

channels.title: Channel Management
channels.loadFailed: Failed to load channels, please try again later
channels.testFailed: Channel test failed, please check your settings
channels.dialog.assignAgentHint: When assigned, this bot only serves that Agent. Leave empty for a global channel.
channels.setup.telegram.step1: 1. Search @BotFather on Telegram and start a chat
channels.setup.telegram.step2: 2. Send /newbot and follow the prompts to set name and username
channels.setup.telegram.step3: 3. BotFather returns a Bot Token — copy and paste it below
channels.setup.telegram.note: Long polling mode — no webhook configuration needed
channels.setup.line.step2: 2. Create a Provider → Messaging API Channel
channels.setup.line.step3: 3. Basic settings → copy the Channel Secret
channels.setup.line.step4: 4. Messaging API → Issue Channel Access Token
channels.setup.line.step5: 5. Webhook settings → set the URL and enable Use webhook
channels.setup.discord.step2: 2. New Application → Bot (left panel) → Reset Token → copy the token
channels.setup.discord.step3: 3. Scroll down on the Bot page → Privileged Gateway Intents:
channels.setup.discord.intentWarning: ⚠️ MESSAGE CONTENT INTENT must be enabled (otherwise the bot cannot receive messages)
channels.setup.discord.step4: 4. OAuth2 (left panel) → URL Generator → check the bot scope
channels.setup.discord.step6: 6. Copy the generated URL → open it in a browser → invite the bot to your server
channels.setup.discord.reinviteTip: 💡 If already invited without sufficient permissions, re-invite using the new URL
channels.setup.slack.step1: 1. Go to api.slack.com/apps → Create New App
channels.setup.slack.step3: 3. Copy the Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...)
channels.setup.slack.step4: 4. Socket Mode → enable → get the App-Level Token (xapp-...)
channels.setup.slack.note: Socket Mode — no public URL required
channels.setup.whatsapp.step2: 2. Create a Business App → add the WhatsApp product
channels.setup.whatsapp.step3: 3. WhatsApp → API Setup → obtain the Access Token
channels.setup.whatsapp.step4: 4. Note down the Phone Number ID
channels.setup.whatsapp.step5: 5. Configuration → set Webhook URL and Verify Token
channels.setup.whatsapp.note: Requires Meta Business verification for production use
channels.setup.feishu.step3: 3. Credentials & Basic Info → obtain App ID and App Secret
channels.setup.feishu.step4: 4. Events & Callbacks → set the Request URL

agents.edit.channelsDesc: Set dedicated bot tokens for this agent. Each configured channel will appear as this agent's own bot. Leave empty to use the global channel.
agents.edit.stickerDesc: Automatically send platform-native stickers or emoji reactions based on conversation emotions. Supports Telegram, LINE, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Feishu, and WebChat.

license.modules.subtitle: Determined by the active tier. This page refreshes automatically after upgrade.
license.feature.prioritySecurityPatch: Priority security patches (8h vs 30d community delay)
license.cta.opensource.title: Upgrade to unlock commercial modules
license.cta.opensource.body: You are running the Apache 2.0 open-source build. All core features are fully usable. A commercial subscription pays for the work of tracking Anthropic / Claude CLI changes, curated industry templates, priority security patches, and Discord support.
license.cta.renew.title: License expiring soon
license.cta.renew.body: Please renew before expiry. Once expired this gateway downgrades to OpenSource mode — core features keep working, commercial modules stop loading.
license.cli.title: Manage your license from the CLI
license.cli.body: Everything on this page is also available through the command line:

reliability.title: Reliability Report
reliability.metric.consistencyScore.desc: How consistent replies are with SOUL.md
reliability.metric.taskSuccessRate.desc: Share of delegated tasks completed successfully
reliability.metric.skillAdoptionRate.des: Share of synthesized skills that graduate to adoption
reliability.metric.fallbackTriggerRate.d: How often the model fallback chain fires (lower is better)
reliability.legend.title: Legend
reliability.legend.note: Lower is better for fallback rate; higher is better elsewhere
users.error.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
webchat.welcome: Hi! How can I help you today?
webchat.hint: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
webchat.visionOnTip: This model can understand uploaded images
webchat.visionOffTip: This model can't understand images; it only reads the text content of documents
webchat.visionWarning: ⚠️ The current model doesn't support image understanding — the image will still be sent, but the model may not be able to see its content.
wikiTrust.title: Wiki Trust
wikiTrust.subtitle: Trust signals and overrides for each agent's wiki pages
agents.cap.desc: Control which tools and computer-use abilities this agent may use. This tab is write-only; unchanged fields are not overwritten.
agents.cap.allowedTools.hint: Whitelist; empty means default policy. Maps to Claude CLI --allowedTools.
agents.cap.deniedTools.hint: Blacklist, e.g. Bash. Maps to Claude CLI --disallowedTools.
agents.cap.wikiVisibleTo.hint: Agents allowed to read this agent's wiki.
agents.contract.desc: Define this agent's behavioral boundaries (CONTRACT.toml). Applies on the next turn.
agents.contract.mustNot.placeholder:
Never drop the production database
Never leak API keys
agents.contract.mustAlways.placeholder:
Confirm before destructive actions
Reply in the user's language
skillSynthesis.desc: Automatically distill high-quality conversations into reusable skills. Runs the Rollout-to-Skill pipeline on a schedule instead of waiting for a manual trigger. Changes take effect within one scheduler poll (~30 min) — no restart needed.
skillSynthesis.autoRun.hint: Off by default. When on, the pipeline runs automatically on the interval below.
skillSynthesis.dryRun.hint: When on, only scores and logs candidates — nothing is written to the Skill Bank.
skillSynthesis.liveWarning: Live mode: synthesized skills will be written to the Skill Bank. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (or [api] anthropic_api_key) to be configured; otherwise the run is skipped.
skillSynthesis.apiKeyHelp: Don't have a key yet? Create one in the Anthropic Console, then add it to config.toml under [api] anthropic_api_key (takes effect without a restart), or set the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.
skillSynthesis.targetAgent.hint: Owner of synthesized skills before graduation. Leave blank to use the default agent.
redaction.desc: Redact and vault sensitive content before data leaves the system.
redaction.toolEgress.hint: Control how redacted arguments are restored per tool call.
mcpKeys.title: MCP Keys
mcpKeys.desc: Manage API keys for external clients accessing the DuDuClaw MCP server. Keys are shown only once at creation.
mcpKeys.created.title: Key Created
mcpKeys.created.warning: Store this key now — it cannot be retrieved again after you close this dialog.
mcpKeys.revoke.title: Revoke MCP Key
mcpKeys.revoke.fullKey.hint: Revoking requires the full key; cleartext is never stored, so paste the complete key.
killswitch.title: Kill Switch
killswitch.desc: Configure triggers and circuit-breaker thresholds to prevent runaway behavior. Most thresholds apply on gateway restart.
agents.runtime.desc: Choose this agent's AI backend and PTY pool settings. Write-only tab — existing config is not overwritten unless you change it.
agents.runtime.fallback.hint: Used when the primary backend fails; pick None to clear.
agents.runtime.pty.hint: Drives the interactive claude REPL for OAuth-subscription accounts. Off by default.
agents.evo.desc: Advanced evolution settings. Write-only tab — existing config is not overwritten unless you change it.
agents.container.advancedDesc: Advanced container settings (worktree / mounts / cmd / env). Write-only — existing config is not overwritten unless you change it.
agents.container.worktreeCopyFiles.hint: Files to copy when creating a worktree, e.g. .env.
agents.container.mounts.hint: Sensitive paths (.ssh / .env / .aws, etc.) are rejected server-side.
agents.container.cmd.hint: Container startup command, added per item.
inference.title: Inference Settings
inference.desc: Configure inference.toml — local LLM backends, generation params, confidence router, and compression.
inference.gen.stop.hint: Generation stop markers, added per item.
inference.router.strongThreshold.hint: Must be less than the Fast threshold.
inference.router.thresholdError: Strong threshold must be less than the Fast threshold.
inference.oc.apiKey.hint: Sent only when you type one; leave blank to keep unchanged.
inference.oc.apiKey.set: A key is set. Leave blank to keep it; type a new value to overwrite.
inference.backend.emptySection: No fields in this section (not yet configured in inference.toml).
gov.title: Governance Policies
gov.desc: Manage rate, permission, quota and lifecycle policies (policies/*.yaml) for global or per-agent scope. Saved policies apply on the next PolicyRegistry reload.
gov.remove.title: Remove Policy
gov.remove.confirm: Delete this policy? This cannot be undone.
gov.field.agentId.hint: * for global, or a valid agent id
scp.desc: Declare who may write each top-level namespace. read_only allows only the named sync source; operator_only is never writable via MCP.
scp.empty: No namespace policies (all default to agent_writable).
scp.error.syncedRequired: read_only mode requires a synced_from value
scp.field.syncedFrom.hint: Only this capability may write, e.g. identity:read
agents.odoo.desc: Per-agent Odoo connection override and permissions. api_key / password are write-only, sent only when typed.
agents.odoo.allowedActions.hint: verb or verb:model, e.g. read or write:crm.lead
agents.odoo.secret.hint: Write-only; leave blank to keep current
agents.adv.desc: Already-supported but scattered advanced fields. Sent only when this tab is edited.
agents.adv.accountPool.hint: Account ids available to this agent
settings.system.restartNote: Changing bind / port / auth_token requires a gateway restart to take effect.
settings.system.writeOnly: Write-only; leave blank to keep current
accounts.field.label: Label
users.remove.confirm: Delete user "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
partner.license.cliOnly: License generation is CLI-only. Run the following command on the server:
welcome.hero.title: 👋 Let's create your first agent
welcome.hero.subtitle: A few quick steps and you'll have your own AI companion. First we'll pick its brain, then give it a name.
welcome.error: Couldn't create the agent. Please try again.
welcome.backend.title: Choose an AI backend
welcome.backend.subtitle: Decide which brain this agent thinks with — you can change it later in settings.
welcome.backend.claudeSub.desc: Use your Claude CLI OAuth subscription — no extra cost.
welcome.backend.claudeApi.desc: Connect directly with an Anthropic API key — great without a subscription.
welcome.backend.genericApi.desc: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint: OpenAI, vLLM, Ollama, llamafile, and more.
welcome.backend.local.desc: Run a GGUF model on this machine — fully offline, no account.
welcome.backend.otherCli.desc: Use the Codex, Gemini, or Antigravity CLI as the backend.
welcome.backend.claudeLoggedIn: Claude is signed in (plan: {plan}) — you're good to go.
welcome.backend.claudeLoginHint: No Claude login detected. Run claude in a terminal to sign in via OAuth first.
welcome.backend.apiKey.optional: Optional — some local endpoints don't need a key
welcome.backend.budget.hint: Calls stop once the limit is reached, so you never overspend.
welcome.backend.keyValidateNote: The key is validated on first use.
welcome.backend.localModel.hint: Name of a downloaded GGUF model.
welcome.backend.manageInInference: You can download and manage local models on the Inference page later.
welcome.identity.title: Give your agent a name
welcome.identity.agentId.hint: Used internally — lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
welcome.identity.trigger.hint: How to summon it in a channel. Leave blank to auto-fill.
welcome.identity.persona.placeholder: Describe this agent's role, tone, and expertise…
welcome.success.title: Your agent is ready!
welcome.success.subtitle: Your first AI companion is all set up.
tour.prompt.title: Want a quick tour?
tour.prompt.body: Take a minute to get to know the key pages. Skip anytime.
tour.step.dashboard.title: Dashboard
tour.step.dashboard.body: An overview of your agents, channels, budget, and system health.
tour.step.webchat.title: Chat
tour.step.webchat.body: Talk to your agent and test its replies right here.
tour.step.agents.title: Agents
tour.step.agents.body: Add, pause, and edit agents, or tune their model and permissions.
tour.step.channels.title: Channels
tour.step.channels.body: Connect your agent to LINE, Telegram, Discord, and more.
tour.step.accounts.title: Accounts & budget
tour.step.accounts.body: Manage API accounts, key rotation, and monthly spend caps.
tour.step.inference.title: Local inference
tour.step.inference.body: Download and configure local GGUF models for offline use.
tour.step.memory.title: Memory
tour.step.memory.body: Browse your agent's episodic and semantic memory.
tour.step.knowledge.title: Knowledge & skills
tour.step.knowledge.body: Maintain the wiki knowledge base and extend your agent's skills.
tour.step.settings.title: Settings
tour.step.settings.body: Tune the gateway, inference mode, and advanced options — and replay this tour anytime.
workspace.subtitle: One line — hand it to your AI employee.
workspace.switchHint: To switch, set the default agent on the Manage page
claw.title: Claw — your first AI employee
claw.subtitle: Runs on your own machine — your data never leaves.
claw.privacy.title: Your machine, privacy first
claw.privacy.desc: Self-hosted single binary; data stays put — not a hosted cloud sandbox
claw.memory.title: Knows you, remembers all
claw.memory.desc: Learns your background, preferences, and habits
claw.channels.title: Gets things done on your channels
claw.superpowers.title: Built-in superpowers
claw.superpowers.desc: Skills, local inference, ERP, browser automation

migrate.platform.hermes.name: Hermes

migrate.platform.paperclip.desc: Import team, tasks and skills. Run an export in paperclip first.
migrate.source.title: Source
migrate.source.label: Source path
migrate.source.help.required: Required for paperclip: point to the folder you just exported.
migrate.source.help.optional: Leave blank to use the platform default path.

migrate.paperclip.exportHint: paperclip keeps its data in an embedded database. Export it with the official command, then enter the folder path below.

migrate.col.name: Name
migrate.notes.title: Notes
migrate.rename.title: Import with auto-rename
migrate.rename.hint: On a name clash, import under a new name instead of skipping. The preview still shows it as skipped; rename applies only on the real run.
migrate.verdict.label: Overall result
migrate.report.title: Full report location
migrate.confirm.title: Start the migration?
migrate.confirm.message: This will write {imported} items from {platform} into DuDuClaw, including new agents, channel setup and skills. Existing settings with the same name are never overwritten.
migrate.applying: Migrating. This can take tens of seconds, please wait…
migrate.error.title: Migration failed

nav.world.desc: Watch your AI staff around the isometric office
growth.subtitle: Your company's level, achievements, and daily reports
growth.unavailable.title: Growth data unavailable
growth.unavailable.desc: Your company's level and achievements appear once the gateway is connected.
growth.achievements.title: Achievement wall
growth.ach.first_agent.name: First teammate
growth.ach.first_task_done.name: First delivery
growth.ach.tasks_100.name: Century
growth.ach.knowledge_100.name: Knowledge base
growth.ach.skills_10.name: Skill master
growth.ach.inbox_zero_streak_7.name: Inbox hero
growth.ach.custom_skill_first.name: Efficiency inventor
growth.ach.custom_skill_first.desc: Got your first custom skill approved and live.
growth.ach.custom_skill_saved_100h.name: Time alchemist
growth.ach.custom_skill_saved_100h.desc: Custom skills saved 100 hours in total.
growth.report.title: Yesterday's report
growth.report.subtitle: Settlement for {date}
growth.report.archive.title: Daily reports
growth.report.empty: No report data for this day.
wip.title: Under construction
wip.taskDetail: The task detail page is coming soon.
wip.skillNew: The build-your-own skill wizard is coming soon.
wip.skillCustom: Self-built skill details are coming soon.
wip.growth: The growth & achievements system is coming soon.
skills.new.title: Build a skill
skills.custom.title: Self-built skill
inbox.failedRun.title: {agent} run failed
inbox.group.label: Group
inbox.sort.label: Sort
inbox.columns.label: Columns
inbox.blocked.inputHint: Staff stuck waiting for more info
inbox.blocked.attentionHint: Unclaimed or stalled — worth a look
inbox.zero.title: Inbox zero — nice ✨
inbox.zero.hint: Nothing needs you right now. Go do something else.
approval.plan.title: What this AI employee plans to do
approval.plan.kind.browser_action: Drive the browser to visit and act on external web pages
approval.plan.kind.tool_call: Call a tool to carry out an action
approval.plan.kind.skill_activation: Activate a skill to handle the task
approval.plan.kind.skill_create: Create and install a new skill (which will run later)
approval.plan.kind.strategic_plan: Propose a plan for your sign-off (nothing runs yet)
approval.plan.kind.agent_hire: Hire a new AI employee (consumes budget)
approval.plan.kind.wiki_ingest: Write content into the shared knowledge base
approval.plan.kind.unknown: Perform an action that hasn't been categorized
approval.confirm.title: High-risk action — confirm once more
approval.confirm.message: This is a high-risk action: {summary}. Approve and let the AI employee run it?
approval.fatigue.nudge: High volume — watch out for rubber-stamping when tired
approval.batch.hint: {count} alike "{kind}" — spot-check one to represent the batch
inbox.keyboardHint: j/k move · Enter open · a archive · U unread · ⌘Z undo
agents.roster.lvBasis: Level derived from {done} completed tasks (+12 XP each)
agentDetail.live.title: Live activity
agentDetail.tasks.title: Recent tasks
world.scene.title: Scene
world.scene.office.name: Office
world.scene.office.desc: An open-plan office: a desk each, ringed by a whiteboard, bulletin board and plants.
world.scene.town.name: Town
world.scene.town.desc: Gridded streets, colourful buildings, cruising cars and a central plaza pool — your staff about town.
world.scene.lounge.name: Lounge
world.scene.lounge.desc: Sofas, a coffee machine and a board-game table where resting staff gather.
world.error.title: The world scene failed to load
world.error.hint: Your device or browser can't show this scene right now — try the list view instead.
world.expand.label: Expand
orgchart.world.title: Full-width world
orgchart.world.desc: See your AI staff walk, interact and work in the immersive isometric office.
chat.employees.title: AI staff
skills.new.subtitle: Describe the skill you want in plain language, let an AI staffer draft it, then send it up for approval
skills.new.describe.label: What skill do you want?
skills.new.describe.help: State the goal and give one example input and expected output
skills.new.describe.placeholder: e.g. Turn each day's support chats into a to-do list — input is a conversation, output is bulleted tasks…
skills.new.builder.label: Which AI staffer builds it
skills.new.builder.help: Pick a staffer to draft the SKILL.md for you
skills.new.builder.none: No staffers yet — create one first
skills.new.generate.readyHint: You can fill in the human-facing fields now
skills.new.generate.reviseHelp: Leave revision notes and the staffer will rewrite a draft
skills.new.generate.revisePlaceholder: e.g. also handle the empty-conversation case…
skills.new.form.displayNamePlaceholder: A human-facing name, e.g. "Support to-do roundup"
skills.new.form.timeSavedHelp: Your own estimate, later used to project cumulative savings
skills.new.form.tagsPlaceholder: Type and press Enter to add
skills.new.review.title: Safety check before submitting
skills.new.review.explain: An injection scan and safety check run automatically before submit; high risk is blocked and the report is attached to the approval
skills.new.submitted.title: Submitted for review
skills.new.submitted.awaiting: It goes live automatically once your manager approves
skills.custom.emptyHint: Describe the skill you want in plain language and let an AI staffer draft it
skills.custom.liveHint: Installed into the real skills directory — your staffers can use it now
skills.custom.generatingHint: The AI staffer is drafting the SKILL.md…
inbox.skillCreate.sandboxSkipped: Sandbox trial is a post-install probation mechanism; not run before submit
inbox.skillCreate.rejectedDone: Rejected — the creator can fix and resubmit
settings.general.gatewayAddress.help: The network address this server listens on (read-only).
settings.general.logLevel.help: How detailed the logs are; raise it when troubleshooting.
settings.general.rotationStrategy.help: When you have multiple accounts, decides the order they're used.
voice.voiceMode.help: When on, the AI replies to you with voice.
voice.asrProvider.help: The service that turns your speech into text.
voice.ttsProvider.help: The service that reads the AI's text reply aloud.
proactive.enabled.help: When on, the AI reaches out on its own instead of only replying when you speak first.
proactive.checkInterval.help2: How often the AI checks whether it should say something.
proactive.quietHours.help: The AI won't reach out during these hours (24-hour clock).
proactive.maxMessagesPerHour.help: Cap on proactive messages per hour, to avoid nagging.
proactive.notifyChannel.help: Which chat platform proactive messages go to.
proactive.chatId.help: The conversation ID on that platform (advanced; usually auto-filled).
settings.cron.desc: Have an AI employee run a task on a fixed schedule, e.g. a daily morning summary.
settings.cron.removeConfirmMsg: Delete “{name}”? This schedule will stop running automatically.
settings.heartbeat.desc: Have AI employees patrol on a timer: check inbox, review to-dos, report back.
settings.heartbeat.enabledHelp: When on, this employee patrols automatically on a timer.
settings.system.desc: This page holds system engineering settings — most users never need to touch it.
settings.system.bind.help: The network interface the server listens on; a wrong value can make it unreachable (needs restart).
settings.system.port.help: The port the server listens on (needs restart).
settings.system.dangerDesc: Changing these needs a restart and may temporarily cut off the dashboard.
settings.system.healthInterval.help: How often each account's health is checked.
settings.system.cooldown.help: How long to rest an account after it hits a rate limit.
settings.system.defaultAgent.help: Who handles requests when no employee is specified.
settings.system.inferenceMode.help: Which brain to use: local model, Claude, or a mix.
settings.system.logFormat.help: The output format of log files.
settings.container.desc: Used to isolate AI employee tasks in a sandbox (detected status below, read-only).
settings.autopilot.desc: Trigger actions automatically on events, e.g. notify an employee when a new task arrives.
settings.skillSynthesis.desc: Let the platform distil new skills from past experience (advanced).
settings.redaction.desc: Automatically mask sensitive data (phone, email) before it reaches the AI.
settings.doctor.desc: One-click health check of your setup, with auto-repair for common issues.
settings.browser.desc: Manage approvals and logs for when AI employees drive a browser or computer (advanced).
security.credentialProxy.title: Credential proxy
security.credentialProxy.desc: Injects credentials via a secure proxy — no secrets are ever exposed to employees.
security.mountGuard.title: Mount guard
security.mountGuard.desc: Container filesystem mount rules — controls which files employees can reach.
security.rbac.title: Role permissions
security.rbac.desc: Role-based control over what each AI employee is allowed to do.
security.rateLimiter.title: Rate limiter
security.rateLimiter.desc: Caps each employee's call rate per time window to prevent runaway usage.
killswitch.maxRepliesPerMinute.help: Emergency-stop if replies per minute exceed this, to stop spam bursts.
killswitch.maxConsecutiveErrors.help: Stop after this many failures in a row to avoid useless retries.
killswitch.errorRateThreshold.help: Stop when the error ratio exceeds this (0 to 1, e.g. 0.5 = half).
killswitch.costLimitUsd.help: Stop once total spend reaches this amount (USD).
mcp.custom.dangerTitle: Custom commands run on your machine
mcp.custom.dangerDesc: The command, arguments, and environment variables below are used to launch a real process on this host. Only enter sources you trust, and double-check every value before you paste it in.
mcp.env.help: One KEY=VALUE per line, split at the first equals sign: the name is before it, everything after is the value. Blank lines are ignored.
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.memory.read: Read an agent's memory (episodic and semantic).
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.memory.write: Write or update an agent's memory.
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.wiki.read: Read pages in the shared knowledge base.
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.wiki.write: Create, edit, or delete shared knowledge base pages.
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.messaging.send: Send outbound messages through connected channels.
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.identity.read: Look up people's identity records (name, role, contact).
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.odoo.read: Read Odoo data (CRM, sales, inventory, accounting).
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.odoo.write: Create or modify records in Odoo.
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.odoo.execute: Run Odoo methods and actions (e.g. confirm an order).
mcpKeys.scopeDesc.admin: Full admin access to every tool and setting. Grant with care.
users.remove.requireHint: Type the user's name to confirm permanent deletion.
inference.advancedNotice: This is an advanced, technical settings page — local inference backends, generation parameters, and routing rules. Most users don't need to touch it.
inference.modelsDir.hint: Folder path where local model files (GGUF) are stored.
inference.defaultModel.hint: Model name loaded by default when none is specified.
inference.maxMemoryMb.hint: Memory ceiling (MB) allowed for local inference; leave blank for no limit.
inference.backend.rawNotice: The fields below map directly to raw config keys — intended only for users familiar with local inference.
gov.field.type.help: What this rule governs: rate, permissions, quota, or lifecycle. Cannot be changed after creation.
gov.rate.resource.help: The action being counted. When it exceeds the count set below, the violation action fires.
gov.rate.limit.help: Maximum times allowed within the time window. Higher is more lenient, lower is stricter.
gov.rate.window.help: The length of the counting window in seconds. For example, 60 means the limit resets every minute.
gov.rate.action.help: What happens when the limit is exceeded: block it, just log a warning, or slow it down.
gov.perm.allowed.help: Only these permissions are allowed; leave empty for no specific restriction. See the example format below.
gov.perm.denied.help: Explicitly forbidden permissions. Anything listed here is blocked even if it also appears in the allow list.
gov.perm.requiresApproval.help: These permissions need human approval before each use — good for high-risk operations.
gov.quota.dailyBudget.help: Total tokens available per day. Once used up it pauses for the day, then resets on the schedule below.
gov.quota.maxTasks.help: Maximum tasks that can run at the same time. Higher is faster but uses more resources.
gov.quota.maxMemory.help: Upper limit on stored memory entries. Enter 0 for no limit.
gov.quota.resetCron.help: Schedule for resetting the quota (cron format). The default 0 0 * * * resets every midnight.
gov.lifecycle.maxIdle.help: Treated as stalled after this many idle hours. Default is 168 hours (7 days).
gov.lifecycle.healthCheck.help: How often to check health, in seconds. Shorter detects issues sooner but checks more often.
gov.lifecycle.autoSuspend.help: Automatically suspend this AI staff member once violations reach this count. Enter 0 to turn this off.
wikiTrust.override.dni.label: Don't inject into AI staff prompt
agents.edit.displayName.help: The name shown in chats and the interface.
agents.edit.icon.help: The emoji or icon shown next to the name.
agents.edit.role.help: Sets this AI staffer's place and responsibilities on the team.
agents.edit.trigger.help: Type this keyword in a channel to summon this staffer.
agents.edit.reportsTo.help: Pick this staffer's manager; delegation and channel tokens walk up this chain.
agents.edit.preferredModel.help: The model used first for day-to-day work.
agents.edit.fallbackModel.help: Used when the preferred model is unavailable.
agents.edit.apiMode.help: Whether to call the model via a subscription account or an API key.
agents.edit.confidenceRouter.help: Automatically routes easy questions locally and hard ones to the cloud.
agents.edit.budgetLimit.help: The monthly spending cap for this staffer.
agents.edit.warnThreshold.help: Warn once spending reaches this percentage.
agents.edit.hardStop.help: Stop entirely when over budget, instead of only warning.
agents.edit.heartbeatEnabled.help: Let this staffer patrol and clear its queue on a schedule.
agents.edit.heartbeatCron.help: Patrol at fixed times; the interval above is used when unset.
agents.edit.sandbox.help: Run tasks in an isolated container so they can't affect the host.
agents.edit.readonlyProject.help: Mount the project read-only so tasks can't change source files.
agents.edit.taskTimeout.help: The longest a single task may run.
agents.edit.maxConcurrent.help: How many tasks may run at the same time.
agents.edit.networkAccess.help: Allow the container to reach the network; tasks can then call external services.
agents.container.danger.title: High risk: host access
agents.container.danger.desc: These settings widen the container's access to the network and host files. Enable with care.
agents.container.worktreeAutoMerge.help: Merge the work branch back automatically when a task finishes, with no manual review.
agents.perm.danger.title: Privilege escalation
agents.perm.danger.desc: These permissions let a staffer self-upgrade or change its own core setup. Confirm before enabling.
agents.edit.canSendCrossAgent.help: Allow this staffer to message other staff.
agents.edit.canModifySkills.help: Allow the staffer to adjust its own installed skills.
agents.edit.canScheduleTasks.help: Allow the staffer to create scheduled tasks itself.
agents.edit.skillSecurityScan.help: Security-scan a skill before installing it.
agents.edit.gvuEnabled.help: Enable the self-reflection and evolution loop.
agents.edit.cognitiveMemory.help: Enable long-term memory that carries key facts across conversations.
agents.edit.canCreateAgents.help: Allow this staffer to create sub-staff of its own.
agents.edit.canModifySoul.help: Allow the staffer to rewrite its own core persona (SOUL).
agents.edit.skillAutoActivate.help: Auto-enable newly synthesized skills, skipping manual approval.
agents.cap.danger.title: Computer Use
agents.cap.danger.desc: These settings let the staffer drive a browser and desktop — a high-risk capability.
agents.cap.computerUse.help: Let the staffer actually operate a computer via a virtual display.
agents.cap.computerUseMode.help: Operate inside an isolated container, or directly on your own machine.
agents.cap.nativeWarning: Direct mode has no container isolation — actions run on your physical computer. Proceed with extra care.
agents.cap.browserViaBash.help: Allow the staffer to drive a Playwright/Puppeteer browser from the command line.
agents.cap.autoConfirmTrusted.help: Auto-approve trusted actions instead of confirming each step.
agents.adv.kv.warning: Free-form values for advanced use only; bad keys may be ignored or cause unexpected behavior.
agentDetail.dismiss.confirm: Dismiss “{name}”? This removes the AI staffer and cannot be undone.
agentDetail.dismiss.requireHint: Type the staff display name “{name}” to confirm.
manage.distributors.desc: Issue and manage white-label distributor keys
about.title: About
about.subtitle: Brand information and software credits
branding.title: Branding
branding.intro: Customize the product name, logo, and company details shown across the dashboard.
branding.locked.title: Branding is locked
branding.locked.body: White-label branding requires distributor authorization. Contact your provider to enable it.
branding.logo.hint: PNG, JPEG, or WebP up to 512 KB.
branding.logo.badType: Unsupported image type. Use PNG, JPEG, or WebP.
branding.logo.tooLarge: The logo must be 512 KB or smaller.
branding.logo.readFailed: Could not read the image file.
branding.subtitle: Subtitle
branding.accent.hint: Pick a brand color — accents across the whole interface follow it.
branding.aboutHtml.hint: Basic HTML is supported (headings, paragraphs, lists, links, images). Content is safety-filtered before it appears on the About page.
branding.aboutHtml.previewEmpty: Type on the left to see a preview.
branding.bundle.desc: Generate a signed branding bundle. Drop branding.bundle.json into a customer's ~/.duduclaw/ folder and that install applies your brand automatically.
distributor.title: Distributors
distributor.subtitle: Issue and manage white-label distributor licenses
distributor.noIssuer.title: No issuer key configured
distributor.noIssuer.hint: Configure an issuer signing key in config.toml before issuing distributor licenses.
distributor.noIssuer.publicUrl: Once public_url is set, issued keys carry their own renewal endpoint — distributors don't even need to set an environment variable.
distributor.bundle.sign.desc: Sign a branding bundle on a distributor's behalf. Use this when the distributor's instance can't reach this gateway — paste their branding config to sign it here.
distributor.bundle.brandingJson.hint: Paste the branding config exported by the distributor (JSON).
distributor.bundle.invalidJson: Invalid branding config JSON — please check the content.
distributor.empty.title: No distributors yet
distributor.empty.hint: Add a distributor to start issuing white-label licenses.
distributor.controlPlane.title: License control-plane
distributor.controlPlane.guide: This gateway can auto-refresh and revoke the keys it issues. Ask each distributor to set this environment variable on their instance so their licenses phone home here (keeping them alive and picking up revocations):
distributor.field.name: Name
distributor.field.fingerprint.hint: The distributor runs `duduclaw license fingerprint` on their machine and sends you the value.
distributor.issue.title: Issue license — {name}
distributor.issue.result.intro: The license was signed. Send this activation string to the distributor.
distributor.issue.result.guide: Ask the distributor to run: duduclaw license activate <blob>
distributor.remove.confirm.title: Remove distributor?
distributor.remove.confirm.body: Remove this distributor from your records. Distributors with active licenses must be revoked first.
distributor.revoke.confirm.title: Revoke license?
distributor.revoke.confirm.body: This marks the license revoked in your records. Remote revocation still requires publishing a CRL — the license stays valid on the distributor's machine until then.
nav.timeline.desc: Every AI staff member's work at a glance
timeline.subtitle: Runs, handoffs and overlaps across your AI staff, laid out in time
timeline.truncated: Display limit reached ({cap} rows) — older records are not shown
timeline.empty: No work recorded in this window
timeline.empty.hint: Try a wider time range, or assign a task first
nav.runs.desc: What each AI staff member actually did, step by step
runs.subtitle: Replay a conversation turn: the request, the tool steps on record, and the reply
runs.empty.hint: Once an AI staff member replies on any channel, the run shows up here
runs.error: Failed to load the run log
runs.steps: {count, plural, one {# tool step} other {# tool steps}}
runs.select: Pick a run on the left
runs.select.hint: The full transcript of that run appears here
runs.transcript.empty: This run has no recorded events
runs.transcript.note: Tool steps and task-board snapshots cover recorded operations only; thinking summaries are not kept.
nav.canvas.desc: Live visual workspace pushed by your AI staff
canvas.subtitle: AI staff push reports, charts and other visuals here, live as the work happens
canvas.empty: This AI staff member hasn't pushed a canvas yet
canvas.empty.hint: Ask them to present a report or chart on the canvas and it will appear here live
canvas.noAgents: No AI staff visible to you yet
canvas.frame.title: Canvas from {agent}
canvas.note: The canvas renders in a protected sandbox; no scripts or commands ever run.
